bodyFields.definition: What it is like to be an organism, the qualitative and subjective nature of conscious experience that is specific to the type of creature.
bodyFields.significance: Central to the mind-body problem; it shows that physical accounts of the organism do not capture the first-person perspective, creating an explanatory gap.

bodyFields.claim: There are facts about subjective experience that cannot be represented or comprehended by human beings due to the limits of our conceptual scheme and nature.
bodyFields.support: Even if humans lasted forever, our structure may not allow us to operate with concepts of the requisite type; the example of bat phenomenology shows that richness of experience does not depend on human describability; the existence of such facts does not require beings who can access them.

bodyFields.definition: A type of perspective from which subjective experiences are accessible; not private to an individual but shareable only by those sufficiently similar to adopt that point of view.
bodyFields.significance: Explains why objective physical descriptions cannot capture subjective experience; only beings capable of adopting the relevant point of view can understand the ascription of experience; the more different the experiencer, the less success one has in understanding their experience.
